calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-28-2026 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court acknowledges the filing made on April 27, 2026. The matter is ordered off calendar.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: A. Datt; Deputy: Not reported (D204).

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-17-2026 Continued To Apr-28-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Troylene Trammell, petitioner in pro per.

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary.

Ms. Trammell requested a continuance to allow additional time to address issues in the petition and reported that she was working with an attorney and communicating with other interested parties.

The matter was continued to April 28, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-07-2026 Continued To Mar-17-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Troylene Trammell, petitioner in pro per.
Eric Trammell, petitioner's son.

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary.

The Court stated it had reviewed the petition and noted that it was incomplete and procedurally deficient, including inconsistencies regarding whether the decedent died testate or intestate. The Court advised petitioner that if a will exists and is being withheld, relief may be sought by filing a petition pursuant to Probate Code section 8201.

Petitioner requested a continuance to allow time to consult with an attorney and to address the deficiencies in the petition. The Court granted the request.

The Court continued the matter to March 17, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow time to consult with an attorney and for the filing of any procedurally appropriate petition.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
